reference_dependency_rule.description: References (terminal nodes) can be dependencies of ANY entity type
reference_dependency_rule.validation: References are always valid dependencies and bypass entity-to-entity dependency rules

reference_description.source-file: Concrete file implementation: path, language, exports, imports, size, hash
reference_description.external-dep: External dependencies: npm packages, system libraries, language built-ins
reference_description.api-surface: Public API: exported symbols, signatures, contracts, stability level
reference_description.import-statement: Import/require relationships: type, source location, usage context
reference_description.type-def: Type definitions: interfaces, protocols, schemas, contracts
reference_description.test-suite: Test coverage: unit tests, integration tests, coverage percentage
reference_description.code-metric: Quality metrics: complexity, maintainability, duplication, debt
reference_description.doc-comment: Documentation: docstrings, comments, API docs, examples
reference_description.build-artifact: Build outputs: compiled files, bundles, source maps, assets
reference_description.dependency-graph: Computed graphs: call graphs, dependency trees, impact analysis
reference_description.product-component: Links code modules to product components they implement in spec-graph.json
reference_description.execution-trace: Step-by-step execution path through module functions with call details
reference_description.call-graph: Function call relationships showing what calls what during runtime
reference_description.control-flow: Conditional logic, branches, loops, and decision points in code
reference_description.data-flow: Data transformations and how data moves through the system
reference_description.side-effect: Observable effects beyond return values: file I/O, state changes, processes
reference_description.error-path: Exception handling, error propagation, and recovery strategies

reference_note.purpose: References are terminal graph nodes containing concrete implementation details
reference_note.schema: Each reference type has flexible schema to match different languages and contexts
reference_note.naming: Reference keys typically match entity keys (module:auth → source-file:auth)
reference_note.usage: Entities define relationships between code concepts, references provide details
reference_note.language_agnostic: Schema works for any language: JS, Python, Go, Rust, Java, etc.

entity_description.module: Set of all modules/files in the codebase (files that export reusable code)
entity_description.package: Set of all packages/directories/namespaces that organize related modules
entity_description.layer: Set of architectural layers defining separation of concerns and dependency flow
entity_description.namespace: Set of all namespaces/scopes that group related symbols
entity_description.interface: Set of all interfaces/protocols/traits defining contracts
entity_description.class: Set of all classes/structs/objects implementing behavior
entity_description.function: Set of all functions/methods/procedures that perform operations

entity_note.schema: Entities have name & description. Module/package entities may include path/package/layer for practical navigation.
entity_note.instances: module:auth, package:api, class:User are instances within entity sets
entity_note.granularity: Choose entity granularity based on what you want to track and validate
entity_note.flexibility: Not all entity types required - use what makes sense for your architecture

module.notes: path/package/layer allow quick navigation without needing separate source-file references

source_file_schema.description: Implementation details for a source code file

fields.file_path: STRING - Relative path from project root
fields.exports: ARRAY<STRING> - Public symbols exported by this file
fields.imports_internal: ARRAY<STRING> - Internal dependencies as entity paths
fields.imports_external: ARRAY<STRING> - External dependencies as reference paths
fields.layer: INTEGER|STRING - Architectural layer (numeric depth or named layer)
fields.category: STRING - Module category based on architecture
fields.loc: INTEGER - Lines of code (excluding comments and whitespace)
fields.hash: STRING - Content hash for change detection

fields.identifier: STRING - Unique package identifier (npm:fs-extra, pypi:requests, crates:tokio)
fields.purpose: STRING - Why this dependency is used
fields.usage_count: INTEGER - Number of modules that import this
fields.security_issues: INTEGER - Known CVEs or security advisories

api_surface_schema.description: Public API exported by a module (language-agnostic)

fields.breaking_changes: ARRAY<STRING> - Known breaking changes from previous versions

fields.symbols: ARRAY<STRING> - Specific symbols imported (empty = entire module)
fields.location: STRING - File and line number where import occurs
fields.usage_context: STRING - How imported symbols are used

test_suite_schema.description: Test coverage and test suite information

fields.cognitive_complexity: INTEGER - How difficult code is to understand
fields.technical_debt_minutes: INTEGER - Estimated time to fix issues

rules.no_upward_dependencies: Layer N cannot depend on layer N+1 or higher
rules.no_circular_dependencies: No cycles allowed in module dependency graph
rules.layer_coherence: Modules in same package should be in same or adjacent layers
rules.external_deps_allowed: All layers can depend on external dependencies

clean_architecture.rules: Inner layers don't depend on outer layers

three_tier.rules: Each layer only depends on layer below

microservices.rules: Services don't depend on each other directly

meta_description.project: Top-level project information: name, language, architecture style
meta_description.conventions: Code conventions, naming patterns, style guides

execution_trace_schema.description: Step-by-step execution path through a module function

step_object.module: STRING - Module or external-dep providing the function
step_object.async: BOOLEAN - Whether this is an async operation

call_graph_schema.description: Function call relationships within and across modules

fields.entry_point: STRING - Function signature that starts the call chain
fields.calls: ARRAY<CALL_OBJECT> - Functions called from entry point

call_object.module: STRING - Module entity path providing the function

control_flow_schema.description: Conditional logic and branching in code

fields.location: STRING - File path and line number
fields.if_true: OBJECT - Actions when condition is true
fields.if_false: OBJECT - Actions when condition is false

fields.operation: STRING - Operation entity path if applicable
fields.reversible: BOOLEAN - Whether effect can be undone
fields.idempotent: BOOLEAN - Whether repeating has same effect
fields.cleanup: STRING - How to reverse the effect

fields.propagates_to: STRING - Where error propagates if not caught
fields.user_visible: BOOLEAN - Whether error is shown to user

product_component_schema.description: Link to product component that this code module implements

fields.graph_path: STRING - Relative path to product graph file
fields.operations: ARRAY<STRING> - Operation entity paths implemented by this module

monorepo.entity_strategy: Use package entities for each workspace, modules for files

microservices.description: Multiple services, each its own codebase
microservices.entity_strategy: Each service gets own graph, link via api-surface references
microservices.validation: Services communicate via defined contracts only

library.entity_strategy: Focus on api-surface and external-dep tracking

notes.names: Entity and reference keys use kebab-case with hyphens
notes.schema: Parent key is unique identifier, no separate id field
notes.entity_types: Use subset that matches your needs: module, package, layer, class, etc
notes.purpose: Universal code architecture graph for any language and architecture style
notes.extensibility: Add custom entity types and references for domain-specific needs
